title: toxicodendron griffithii var. microcarpum
intro: Toxicodendron griffithii var. microcarpum, also known as the Chinese poison ivy or poison oak, is a plant species in the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its shiny green leaves and small white or yellowish flowers. The plant contains urushiol, an allergenic and irritating sap that causes skin irritation, but it also has medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution must be exercised due to its allergenic properties.
